risk_if_not_implemented: Without Documented Justification For Material Risk Management Decisions, security risks may go unmanaged, resulting in uncontrolled exposures and misaligned security investments.

compensating_control_1.name: Security, Compliance & Resilience Controls Oversight
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to provide a security, compliance and resilience controls oversight function that reports to the organization's executive leadership.
compensating_control_1.justification: Security, Compliance & Resilience Controls Oversight (CPL-02) provides resilience and recovery capability that compensates for the absence of Documented Justification For Material Risk Management Decisions (RSK-13.2) by ensuring the organization can restore operations and data when the primary control is absent. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Executive Leadership Approval For Managing Material Risk
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to obtain executive leadership approval for risk management decisions involving material risk.
compensating_control_2.justification: Executive Leadership Approval For Managing Material Risk (RSK-13) provides risk identification and prioritization that compensates for the absence of Documented Justification For Material Risk Management Decisions (RSK-13.2) by enabling informed decisions about where to focus resources to manage residual exposure. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
